name: The Bhavnagar foundation Gir (India → Brazil)
bloodline_detail: Gir cattle from the Kathiawar peninsula (Bhavnagar / the Gir hills, Gujarat), the base stock for the Brazilian Gyr.
importer: Brazilian ranchers; foundation gift attributed to the Maharaja of Bhavnagar
notable: The Indian foundation from which Brazil's entire dairy-Gir and Girolando industry grew. Tradition names the bull 'Krishna', reportedly among Gir gifted in the 1940s by Krishna Kumarsinhji Bhavsinhji, Maharaja of Bhavnagar, with the widely-repeated claim that a large share of Brazil's Gir descends from him.
notes: Foundation-event narrative, not a single registered animal. The 'Krishna' story and '~80% descend' figure come from secondary/popular Indian sources — treat as tradition, not verified against primary Brazilian import records.

name: Jaguar TE do Gavião
bloodline_detail: Sire Planalto TE (CAL4738), dam Dengosa (MUT14); born 22 Mar 1999.
notable: The most influential modern dairy-Gir foundation sire — sire of world-record milk cows Helga, Bandeira, Jiba, Iname, and Sampa, and of champions Índia, Celeuma, Vaidosa, and Mitra. A top PNMGL bull noted for outstanding fertility.
notes: Registration GAV291 and parentage shown on a public breeding-database page — verified public.

name: Fábrica FIV de Brasília
bloodline_detail: Matriarch of the modern Cabo Verde / Brasília export dynasty; cloned to preserve her genetics.
notable: Historic matriarch of the modern dairy-Gir export lines and the first dairy Gir cow to exceed 60 kg of milk per day (ExpoZebu, May 2013). Her descendants (Amora, Honesta, Broinha and others) anchor the highest-value export donor herds.
notes: Records via Canal do Leite; registration number not published. Cloned as Fábrica TN1 Cabo Verde.

name: Antonione FIV Cabo Verde
bloodline_detail: From the Cabo Verde herd; a leading modern progeny-test and export sire.
notable: The leading modern AI export sire: PTA milk of +936 kg, more than 150,000 doses commercialised (~2,000/month), and leader of the 1st genomic group (2023) and 32nd PNMGL progeny-test group. Daughters noted for high milk, body capacity, and udders.
notes: Registration number not confirmed on a public page. Breeder Fazenda São José do Can Can (MG); Colombian partner herds. Proofs published via ABS Global / PNMGL.

name: Iluminada Teatro FIV do Fundão
bloodline_detail: Owned by Fazenda do Fundão, Pains (MG).
notable: World single-day milk record: 80.15 kg in one day at the 42nd Milk Competition of the 87th ExpoZebu (Uberaba, 2022), in her 4th lactation at age 8 — surpassing the 2019 record of 65.88 kg/day.
notes: Owner Roberto Menezes Horta; registration number not published.

name: Helga FIV TOL
bloodline_detail: Daughter of Jaguar TE do Gavião.
notable: Held the world single-day milk record at 78.37 kg/day (EXPOGIL 2017, Uberaba), surpassing three prior records held by her stablemate Bandeira; later exceeded by Iluminada in 2022.
notes: Owner Maria Tereza Lemos Tóla, Fazenda Paraíso; registration number not published.

name: Bandeira
bloodline_detail: Daughter of Jaguar TE do Gavião.
notable: Held three successive world milk records before Helga; a young-cow world record of ~63.2 kg/day in 2014.
notes: From Fazenda Paraíso; registration number not published.

name: Broinha FIV Cabo Verde
bloodline_detail: From the Cabo Verde export dynasty (Fábrica line).
notable: The world's most valuable dairy Gir cow — a R$5.04 million valuation (50% sold for R$2.52M at Mutum Weekend, Sept 2025) — plus a young-cow world milk record of ~64.84 kg/day. A signal of the export-embryo premium on this line.
notes: Buyer Grumarim Agropecuária / Gir São Francisco de Assis; registration number not published.

name: Magda Jirasol Gengis Khan N.T
notable: Held the world's-most-valuable-Gir title before Broinha: R$3.36M (50% sold for R$1.68M to Colombian investors, May 2025) — a direct signal of the LatAm export-embryo premium.
notes: Records via CompreRural; registration number not published.

name: Espetáculo FIV
notable: One of four dairy-Gir sires whose semen made up Brazil's largest-ever bovine-genetics export — 40,000 doses to India's NDDB (Mother Dairy), January 2024, via Alta Genetics.
notes: Named in the ABCZ news item on the India export; registration number not published.

name: Haroldo FIV da Genipapo
bloodline_detail: Owned by Paulo Roberto de Andrade Cunha.
notable: An export sire in the January-2024 Alta Genetics 40,000-dose dairy-Gir shipment to India's NDDB.
notes: Named in the ABCZ news item on the India export; registration number not published.

name: Ivã FIV de Brasília
bloodline_detail: From Fazenda Brasília; co-owned by Luiz Eduardo Branquinho and Alta Genetics do Brasil.
notable: An export sire in the January-2024 Alta Genetics 40,000-dose dairy-Gir shipment to India's NDDB.
notes: Named in the ABCZ news item on the India export; registration number not published.

name: Trovão FIV S. Edwiges
bloodline_detail: Owned by José Maria de Souza and Alta Genetics do Brasil.
notable: An export sire in the January-2024 Alta Genetics 40,000-dose dairy-Gir shipment to India's NDDB.
notes: Named in the ABCZ news item on the India export; registration number not published.

name: Gil FIV Cabo Verde
bloodline_detail: A proven son of Antonione FIV Cabo Verde.
notable: A proven Antonione son used heavily in IVF (FIV) embryo production — a next-generation node in the Cabo Verde export donor lines.
notes: Mentioned in the ABS Global article on Antonione; registration number not published.

name: Amora FIV Cabo Verde
bloodline_detail: Daughter of the matriarch Fábrica FIV de Brasília.
notable: Led the PNMGL Genomic Evaluation in 2019 with a lactation of 15,138 kg of milk; a high-value donor in the export lines.
notes: Figures via Canal do Leite; registration number not published.

name: Cheiadegraça Rib Grande F.
bloodline_detail: Competed at the 91st ExpoZebu milk competition (2026).
notable: Young-cow world milk record at the 91st ExpoZebu (2026): 64.213 kg/day average, 192.64 kg accumulated over the competition. Also the subject of a headline R$3.99M valuation.
notes: Reported via Agrolink/CompreRural; the primary ABCGIL scoresheet was not fetched — treat kg figures as press-reported. Registration number not published.

name: Profana de Brasília
notable: Cited as a full-lactation world record for Gir at 17,120 kg in a single lactation.
notes: Lactation figure appears in a secondary news source and was NOT verified against a controle-leiteiro / ABCGIL primary record — treat as unverified.

name: Marília FIV Teatro de Naylo
bloodline_detail: Girolando cow (Gir × Holstein); competed at the 34th Torneio Leiteiro, Areias, São Paulo.
notable: Guinness World Record holder for the largest 24-hour milk yield from a cow: 127.57 kg (123.61 L) on 3 August 2019 — the headline demonstration of what Gir-based tropical dairy genetics can reach.
notes: Record verified via Guinness World Records. Girolando (not pure Gir), included as the breed's most famous production milestone.
